fields.description:
## Why
Audit Reliability finding #4 (MEDIUM). In `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` `stage_and_commit`, `git add` (~L127-131) and `git commit` (~L132-136) run back-to-back and the failure paths only `_silent_unlink(final_path)` (the worktree file) — they never unstage the blob. So a failed write leaves its event **staged in the index**, and the next successful write (which commits the whole index) durably commits that phantom event. The claim/transition path already fixed this exact bug in `_commands/txn.py::_unstage` (~L98-114, `git reset -q -- <rels>`), called from its failure handlers (~L259, ~L441); the general append path never got the fix. Related (audit 2.2): `git commit` currently runs even when `git add` failed, because the add return code isn't checked before commit.

## What / files
- `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`: (1) check `add.returncode` and raise BEFORE running `git commit` (so a failed add can't let commit sweep unrelated staged residue under this write's message); (2) in both failure branches (add-failed ~L138 and unhealed commit-failed ~L157), run `git reset -q -- <relative_path>` (mirroring `txn._unstage`) in addition to `_silent_unlink(final_path)`, so the blob leaves the index as well as disk.
- Regression test in `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` (or a focused unit test): monkeypatch/inject a `git commit` failure on write A, then perform a successful write B, and assert (a) `git diff --cached` is empty after A fails and (b) replay/fsck contains only B's event, not A's phantom.

## Scope
In: event_append.py failure path + the add-rc-before-commit ordering; one regression test. Out: txn.py (already correct); any broader fsck redesign.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On a stage/commit failure the event is removed from the index (`git reset`) as well as from disk (`_silent_unlink`) — asserted by `git diff --cached` being empty after an injected failure
- [ ] `git add`'s return code is checked and raises before `git commit` is invoked (verified by a test where add fails and commit is never reached / no commit is produced)
- [ ] Regression test: injected commit failure on write A followed by successful write B ⇒ store replay contains only B; fsck is clean
- [ ] `make test` green
